content.thinking:
I found the problem! The `rabbitmq-cookie-perpod` secret shows:
- `rabbitmq-0`: `c3VwZXJzZWNyZXRjb29raWU=` (base64) → `supersecretcookie`
- `rabbitmq-1`: `c3VwZXJzZWNyZXRjb29raWU=` (base64) → `supersecretcookie`
- `rabbitmq-2`: `ZHJpZnRlZGNvb2tpZQ==` (base64) → `driftedcookie`
So `rabbitmq-2` has a different Erlang cookie than the other two nodes, which would prevent it from joining the cluster even though the cluster status shows all three as running. This mismatch is likely why the node fails to rejoin after maintenance. So if `rabbitmq-2` gets restarted during maintenance, it'll pull that mismatched cookie from the secret and won't be able to rejoin the cluster. I need to check what cookie each pod is currently using and trace how the StatefulSet is pulling it from the secret.

content.text: Found the root cause. `rabbitmq-2` has a different Erlang cookie (`driftedcookie`) while the others use `supersecretcookie`. Let me verify the current cookies in use and the StatefulSet configuration.

content.thinking:
All 3 pods have `RABBITMQ_ERLANG_COOKIE=supersecretcookie` as env var. But their on-disk cookies are different. This is very strange.

Let me check if maybe there's an init container that's overwriting the cookie on disk.

Actually, wait. The `emptyDir` volume starts fresh on pod creation. RabbitMQ will write the cookie from `RABBITMQ_ERLANG_COOKIE` env var to `.erlang.cookie` at startup if the file doesn't exist. But the cookies on disk don't match.

Unless... 

Maybe the cookies were modified after the pods started—someone could have exec'd in and changed the files, or a sidecar process altered them. But the cluster still shows as healthy with all nodes running, which doesn't make sense if the cookies were actually different. Let me check if there's a DaemonSet or another Job that might be changing the cookie files post-startup. I'm wondering if the adversary injected a script that modifies the cookies, making this scenario more complex than it first appears. Looking at the actual cookie values from the pods to see what's really going on. The entrypoint script writes the env var to `.erlang.cookie` before RabbitMQ starts, but if the file already exists with different content, it might not overwrite. With an emptyDir volume, the file shouldn't persist between restarts, so the env var should always write the same cookie. But if someone manually modified the cookie files via `kubectl exec` after the pods started, that would explain the different cookies now—though it's strange the cluster appears functional with three different cookies, which shouldn't work with Erlang's distribution protocol. I'm wondering if RabbitMQ is using a different clustering mechanism or if the cookies are being read in an unexpected way. The real issue will surface when `rabbitmq-2` restarts—it'll come back with the mismatched cookie on disk, causing the other nodes to reject its connection attempts. But there's a wrinkle: if the container restarts while the pod stays alive, the emptyDir volume persists, so the corrupted cookie file remains. I need to clarify whether a container restart clears the emptyDir or if that only happens on pod deletion. So if the container restarted without deleting the pod, rabbitmq-2 would pick up the modified cookie and fail to rejoin. Looking at the logs though, rabbitmq-2 appears to have successfully joined the cluster, which suggests the maintenance already happened and it rejoined. But the scenario is still marked as unfixed, so I need to check what the oracle is actually validating. Looking more closely at the per-pod secret, I notice the cookies stored there don't match either the expected `supersecretcookie` or the `driftedcookie` value, which makes me wonder if this secret is even relevant to the actual problem.

I'm seeing a permissions error in the rabbitmq-2 logs about queue access, but that's likely just from the producer app trying to connect before setup completed. Let me step back and check the actual cluster status directly—all three nodes are showing as both disk nodes and running nodes, so they're all currently in the cluster. The real question is whether there's something more subtle going on, like one node being in maintenance mode or having some other issue preventing full membership.

# rabbitmq/failover
#
# A 3-node RabbitMQ cluster is running but one node has failed to rejoin.
# The agent must restore full cluster membership without data loss using
# only native Kubernetes resources — no CRDs, operators, or third-party
# controllers.

prompt: |
  A 3-node RabbitMQ cluster is running in the ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} namespace,
  but one node does not rejoin after maintenance.

  Restore full 3-node membership without data loss using only native
  Kubernetes resources (StatefulSet, Service, PVC, Secret, ConfigMap, etc).
  Do not use any CRD, Operator, or third-party controller.

  Goal:
  - All three RabbitMQ nodes are members of the same cluster.
  - Cluster is healthy and stable.

  Cluster context:
    Namespace: ${BENCH_NAMESPACE}
    kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get pods
    kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} logs <pod>
    kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} exec {{params.cluster_prefix}}-0 -- rabbitmqctl cluster_status
    kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get secret {{params.cluster_prefix}}-cookie-perpod -o yaml

params:
  cluster_prefix:
    default: rabbitmq

namespace_contract:
  required_roles:
    - default

metrics:
  - time_to_first_mutation
  - read_write_ratio
  - blast_radius
  - destructive_ops
  - decoy_integrity
  - residual_drift

preconditionUnits:
  - name: failover_secrets_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'set -e; kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get secret {{params.cluster_prefix}}-cookie-perpod >/dev/null; kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get secret {{params.cluster_prefix}}-admin >/dev/null'"
        timeout_sec: 20
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} create secret generic {{params.cluster_prefix}}-cookie-perpod --from-literal={{params.cluster_prefix}}-0=supersecretcookie --from-literal={{params.cluster_prefix}}-1=supersecretcookie --from-literal={{params.cluster_prefix}}-2=driftedcookie --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -'"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} create secret generic {{params.cluster_prefix}}-admin --from-literal=username=admin --from-literal=password=adminpass --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -'"
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'set -e; kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get secret {{params.cluster_prefix}}-cookie-perpod >/dev/null; kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get secret {{params.cluster_prefix}}-admin >/dev/null'"
        timeout_sec: 20

  - name: rbac_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get sa {{params.cluster_prefix}}"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "envsubst '$BENCH_NAMESPACE $BENCH_NS_SOURCE $BENCH_NS_TARGET $BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_BLUE_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_GREEN_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_FROM_VERSION' < cases/rabbitmq/failover/resource/rbac.yaml | kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} apply -f -"
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get sa {{params.cluster_prefix}}"
        timeout_sec: 10

  - name: config_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get configmap {{params.cluster_prefix}}-config"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "envsubst '$BENCH_NAMESPACE $BENCH_NS_SOURCE $BENCH_NS_TARGET $BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_BLUE_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_GREEN_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_FROM_VERSION' < cases/rabbitmq/failover/resource/configmap.yaml | kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} apply -f -"
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get configmap {{params.cluster_prefix}}-config"
        timeout_sec: 10

  - name: services_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'set -e; kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get svc {{params.cluster_prefix}} >/dev/null; kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get svc {{params.cluster_prefix}}-headless >/dev/null'"
        timeout_sec: 20
    apply:
      - command: "envsubst '$BENCH_NAMESPACE $BENCH_NS_SOURCE $BENCH_NS_TARGET $BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_BLUE_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_GREEN_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_FROM_VERSION' < cases/rabbitmq/failover/resource/services.yaml | kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} apply -f -"
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'set -e; kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get svc {{params.cluster_prefix}} >/dev/null; kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get svc {{params.cluster_prefix}}-headless >/dev/null'"
        timeout_sec: 20

  - name: rabbitmq_statefulset_present
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get sts {{params.cluster_prefix}}"
        timeout_sec: 20
    apply:
      # Composition-tolerance: an inherited StatefulSet from an earlier workflow stage
      # may differ in immutable fields (serviceName/selector/volumeClaimTemplates), which
      # makes `kubectl apply` fail with "Forbidden: updates to statefulset spec ... are
      # forbidden". Orphan-delete the existing STS first so it can be recreated --
      # --cascade=orphan PRESERVES the running pods and the data-* PVCs (and their data),
      # so this is additive and never wipes inherited broker state.
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} delete statefulset {{params.cluster_prefix}} --cascade=orphan --ignore-not-found=true"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "envsubst '$BENCH_NAMESPACE $BENCH_NS_SOURCE $BENCH_NS_TARGET $BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_BLUE_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_GREEN_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_FROM_VERSION' < cases/rabbitmq/failover/resource/statefulset.yaml | kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} apply -f -"
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get sts {{params.cluster_prefix}}"
        timeout_sec: 20

  - name: rabbitmq_cluster_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'set -e; [ \"$(kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get sts {{params.cluster_prefix}} -o jsonpath=\"{.status.readyReplicas}\")\" = \"3\" ]'"
        timeout_sec: 20
    apply:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} rollout status statefulset/{{params.cluster_prefix}} --timeout=600s"
        sleep: 1
    verify:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c 'set -e; [ \"$(kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} get sts {{params.cluster_prefix}} -o jsonpath=\"{.status.readyReplicas}\")\" = \"3\" ]'"
        timeout_sec: 20

  - name: curl_test_ready
    on_probe_fail: skip
    probe:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=curl-test --timeout=3s"
        timeout_sec: 10
    apply:
      - command: "envsubst '$BENCH_NAMESPACE $BENCH_NS_SOURCE $BENCH_NS_TARGET $BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_BLUE_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_GREEN_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_FROM_VERSION' < cases/rabbitmq/failover/resource/curl-test.yaml | kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} apply -f -"
        sleep: 1
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=curl-test --timeout=300s"
        sleep: 20
    verify:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=curl-test --timeout=3s"
        timeout_sec: 10

  - name: failover_baseline_ready
    on_probe_fail: error
    probe:
      - command: "python3 cases/rabbitmq/failover/setup_precondition_check.py --namespace ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} --min-ready 3"
        timeout_sec: 40
    apply:
      - command: "/bin/sh -c true"
        timeout_sec: 5
    verify:
      - command: "python3 cases/rabbitmq/failover/setup_precondition_check.py --namespace ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} --min-ready 3"
        timeout_sec: 40

oracle:
  verify:
    before_commands:
      - command: "envsubst '$BENCH_NAMESPACE $BENCH_NS_SOURCE $BENCH_NS_TARGET $BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_BLUE_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_GREEN_CLUSTER_PREFIX $BENCH_PARAM_FROM_VERSION' < cases/rabbitmq/common/oracle-client.yaml | kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} apply -f -"
        timeout_sec: 30
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} wait --for=condition=ready pod/oracle-client --timeout=60s"
        timeout_sec: 70
    after_commands:
      - command: "kubectl -n ${BENCH_NAMESPACE} delete -f cases/rabbitmq/common/oracle-client.yaml --ignore-not-found=true"
        timeout_sec: 30
    after_failure_mode: warn
    commands:
      - command: "python3 cases/rabbitmq/failover/oracle/oracle.py"
        timeout_sec: 120

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}
spec:
  serviceName: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}-headless
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}
      initContainers:
        - name: copy-cookie
          image: busybox:1.36
          env:
            - name: POD_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
          command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              set -e
              cp /cookie-src/${POD_NAME} /cookie-dst/.erlang.cookie
              chmod 400 /cookie-dst/.erlang.cookie
              chown 999:999 /cookie-dst/.erlang.cookie
          volumeMounts:
            - name: cookie-src
              mountPath: /cookie-src
            - name: erlang-cookie
              mountPath: /cookie-dst
      containers:
        - name: rabbitmq
          image: rabbitmq:3.12-management
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5672
              name: amqp
            - containerPort: 15672
              name: management
          env:
            - name: POD_NAME
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.name
            - name: POD_NAMESPACE
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.namespace
            - name: RABBITMQ_USE_LONGNAME
              value: "true"
            - name: RABBITMQ_NODENAME
              value: "rabbit@$(POD_NAME).${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}-headless.$(POD_NAMESPACE).svc.cluster.local"
            - name: RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_USER
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}-admin
                  key: username
            - name: RABBITMQ_DEFAULT_PASS
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}-admin
                  key: password
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/rabbitmq/${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}.conf
              subPath: rabbitmq.conf
            - name: config
              mountPath: /etc/rabbitmq/enabled_plugins
              subPath: enabled_plugins
            - name: data
              mountPath: /var/lib/rabbitmq
            - name: erlang-cookie
              mountPath: /var/lib/rabbitmq/.erlang.cookie
              subPath: .erlang.cookie
              readOnly: true
          startupProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["rabbitmq-diagnostics", "-q", "check_running"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 120
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["rabbitmq-diagnostics", "-q", "check_running"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 5
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 12
          livenessProbe:
            exec:
              command: ["rabbitmq-diagnostics", "-q", "ping"]
            initialDelaySeconds: 180
            periodSeconds: 30
            timeoutSeconds: 20
            failureThreshold: 10
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}-config
            items:
              - key: rabbitmq.conf
                path: rabbitmq.conf
              - key: enabled_plugins
                path: enabled_plugins
        - name: cookie-src
          secret:
            secretName: ${BENCH_PARAM_CLUSTER_PREFIX}-cookie-perpod
        - name: erlang-cookie
          emptyDir: {}
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 1Gi
